title: Call of Duty

notes:
Hi there, I'd be able to offer a bit of explanation as to why MWII (and eventually MWIII) will not start. 

Activision has began using a custom made Kernel-level Anti-Cheat, known as Ricochet Anti-Cheat. This has become standard since the 2019 reboot of the Modern Warfare series. 
The Anti-Cheat uses elements only found in the Windows kernel, and Ricochet is NOT cross-compatible with the UNIX Kernel/Shell, which Linux is built off of. 

However, not all hope is lost. 

Infinity Ward, the main developer of the game, has mentioned that, if someome manages to find a workaround, they will not be punished for using it, but under some conditions:

1. You cannot impede on the Anti-Cheat's function.
2. Altering any base game files or files relating to the Anti-Cheat is prohibited.
3. You CANNOT use said workaround to cheat. 

I will go ahead and mention that while you can *technically* boot into the game's menu, it will immediately hang the game's process and crash, at least on Arch.

To any of you seeking to find a workaround, best of luck with finding one.
